title: My Mothering Is Mine
subtitle: An affirmation for when comparison with other mothers is loud
excerpt: A short affirmation for the moment comparison with another mother produces the feeling that you are doing it wrong. Rejects the idea that there is one correct way to mother and gives the reader their own authority back.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, drawing on maternal-identity literature on the diversity of good-enough mothering.

content.text: A short affirmation for the comparison spiral: the moment someone else's parenting makes yours feel insufficient. Three words. One complete thought.
Say it once, slowly. Then say it again. Three times is enough.

Your mothering is shaped by your children, your history, and what you have in you today. Another mother's approach cannot be a valid measure of yours; the situations are too different. The affirmation names that: the comparison was never a fair one.

Written for homemade.education. The idea draws on the good-enough-mother tradition in maternal research: what children need is a mother who responds to them, not one who scores well against an outside checklist.
